No opposition filed and good cause shown per CRC 2.550 et seq. rulings.Case Title: JOCELYN WICKER VS. SAN FRANCISCO ANIMAL CARE AND CONTROL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandat
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2018, Line 2, PETITIONER JOCELYN WICKER's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandat Jocelyn Wicker's petition for writ of mandate is DENIED, for several reasons. Wicker fails to present any competent evidence that she lacked notice of the subject hearing. Her petition is unverified and she fails to provide a declaration in compliance with CCP §2015.5 attesting to lack of notice. Respondent's proof of service of the hearing notice - executed under penalty of perjury - and the payment stub Wicker attached to her petition indicate that the notice was served at the correct address (255 San Carlos St., San Francisco, CA 94110). (AR 000007.) Wicker filed no memorandum of points and authorities, which is construed as an admission that the petition lacks merit. (See CRC 3.113(a).) The petition cites CCP §473.5, but that provision applies to civil actions, not administrative proceedings. Wicker's credibility is dubious, as the record shows she gave a false name when her dog Fezzik attacked a retriever named Banjo. (AR 000074, 94.) Substantial evidence supports respondent's decision, which merely requires Wicker to attend dog-obedience school and does not mandate destruction of Fezzik. (See Zuniga v. San Mateo Dept. of Health Services (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1521, 1530.) Wicker has not been diligent. Her July 9, 2018 e-mail shows she received a call regarding the hearing on May 31, 2018 - the day the hearing occurred. Wicker failed to seek any relief from respondent pursuant to Gov't Code §11520. Rather, Wicker waited until September 12, 2018 to file her petition; such a delay is prejudicial. Finally, Wicker appears to concede that Fezzik was involved in another altercation with another dog, Otis, and that alone supports respondent's decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: KING SECURITY SERVICES, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO CULTURAL ARTS TRADITIONS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALINA LAGUNA's Motion To Tax Costs Defendant Alina Laguna's motion to tax costs is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff seeks $8,883 in attorney and legal assistant fees for post-judgment collection efforts. (See Rosen v. LegacyQuest (2014) 225 Cal.App.4th 375.) On a prior costs motion, Judge Kahn taxed plaintiff's attorney fee claim, reducing $7,280 to $2,000. Having reviewed the time records, I find a similar reduction appropriate here: the $8,883 is reduced to $2,500. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Laguna is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Orders, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Two Properties Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Xelan Prop I, Llc]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 20, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA'S Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Orders, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Two Properties Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Xelan Prop I, Llc] Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing continued to December 11, 2018 at 9 am in Dept 301. The Court is concerned about the need to avoid inconsistent judicial recommendation/rulings since there are pending three motions on very similar issues. Specifically, a hearing was held on November 14, 2018 before JPT Cohen for which he is writing a report to Judge Quidachay. The Court believes that any ruling on the motion scheduled for November 20th should await a ruling from Judge Quidachay. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. VS. HENRY HUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2018, Line 6, CROSS DEFENDANTs EMILY WONG, FRANK WONG, KAREN LIU, and ANGEL MEI LIU's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Cross-defendants Emily Wong, Frank Wong, Karen Liu and Angel Liu's motion for relief from default is GRANTED. The motion was "made on the ground that the defaults were entered as a result of the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise or neglect." Thus, attorney Jeffrey Kirschenbaum shall "pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs to opposing counsel or parties." (See CCP §473(b).) I find the reasonable amount of fees and costs to be $15,000. Unreasonable amounts include those described in paragraphs 19 and 20 of attorney Richard Munzinger's declaration; that legal work could have been done in half the claimed time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for cross-defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: MAYRA PEREZ ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A GOVERNMENTAL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 20, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR to give counsel an opportunity to comply with CCP §430.41. That law requires counsel to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed, and attorney Hannawalt's declaration indicates that has not occurred. (Id. at 3.) The parties are ordered to meet and confer and CCSF's time to file a responsive pleading is extended to December 20, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
